sourceMovie.title: Ant-Man
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Ant-Man (2015) stuck because it commits to its science fiction premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Peyton Reed's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The science fiction and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Avengers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ant-Man clicked for you, The Avengers (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Avengers: Infinity War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ant-Man clicked for you, Avengers: Infinity War (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ant-Man?
faq.answer: Try The Avengers, Avengers: Infinity War, Guardians of the Galaxy—each shares DNA with Ant-Man while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ant-Man?
faq.answer: Ant-Man (2015) is best described as Science Fiction, Adventure, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ant-Man part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Ant-Man Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
